candidate: L10 DAILY-PEAD EFFECT-SIZE RECOVERY BOUND (SIGN + MAGNITUDE). SYNTHESIS/feasibility track: NO new edge, NO new data acquisition, NO optimization. Reads the EXISTING earnings + price panels and the FROZEN L3 (monthly-PEAD) + L8 (power) + L9 (volume) results to bound what a day-stamped daily-PEAD effect would need to be -- in SIGN and MAGNITUDE -- to clear the L8 |t|=2 band at the L9-measured volume. Completes the forward-data #1 feasibility loop: L8 = n_required, L9 = volume/rate, L10 = effect-size + the SIGN hurdle.
purpose: L8 quantified the n_required band for |t|=2; L9 showed the real liquid volume reaches it in ~1-8 yr. The remaining unknown is the EFFECT SIZE: a day-stamped daily-PEAD test needs a per-event effect large enough that effect*sqrt(n) >= 2*sd. L10 measures the per-event cross-sectional return dispersion (sd_event) from the real liquid SUE-testable panel and the MONTHLY-resolution SUE effect sign at event level, then computes (a) the per-event |effect| required for |t|=2 at the L9 rate over 1/3/5/8 yr, (b) the recovery factor that day-stamping must achieve over the monthly effect, and (c) whether the monthly LIQUID sign forces day-stamping to FLIP sign (sign-contingent feasibility) rather than merely amplify.
inputs_FROZEN: data/snapshots/earnings_dates.parquet (SUE-nonnull testable events, 2019+; announce_month + consume_from_month month-resolution, look-ahead-safe)
data/clean_universe/adjusted_prices_2019_2026.parquet (adjusted_close for month returns, value_tl for liquidity)

lab-demo-goal/results/l3_pead_results.json (FROZEN monthly-PEAD: K=3 LIQUID long-tercile costfree mean=-0.013155 nw_t=-3.237 sign_stable=true; L-S spread K=1 LIQUID=-0.032 nw_t=-2.91; PEAD-NOT-TRADEABLE)

method_FROZEN.window: fiscal_year >= 2019, SUE non-null (same testable subset as L9; matches the price panel and L3).
method_FROZEN.per_event_forward_return: for each event, the symbol's calendar-month TOTAL return during consume_from_month (look-ahead-safe: the first month tradeable), measured as adjusted_close[last trading day of consume-month] / adjusted_close[last trading day of prior month] - 1. MARKET-RELATIVE: subtract the equal-weighted mean consume-month return of the LIQUID universe that same month (carry-immune PRIMARY series, consistent with L2/L3/H2b).
method_FROZEN.liquidity: per (symbol, consume_month): trailing-63-trading-day median value_tl >= 1.0e7 TL as of the consume-month's first available trailing window (look-ahead-safe, reuse L9 monthly_liquidity keyed to consume_month). LIQUID subset is the primary lens (the only power-relevant universe per L7).
method_FROZEN.sue_sign_effect: split events by SUE sign (positive-SUE sue>0 vs negative-SUE sue<0). MONTHLY event-level SUE effect = mean(market-relative consume-month return | positive-SUE) - mean(... | negative-SUE), computed for ALL and for LIQUID. Reported with event-clustered-by-month mean and a simple t. This is the SIGN anchor (corroborates L3's negative liquid PEAD at event resolution).
method_FROZEN.sd_event: cross-sectional sd of the LIQUID market-relative consume-month per-event returns. This is the noise term in t = effect*sqrt(n)/sd_event.
method_FROZEN.required_effect: for n in {bounded_date_clusters_per_year (L9) * H} for H in {1,3,5,8} yr: required per-event |effect| for |t|=2 is eff_req(n) = T_SIG * sd_event / sqrt(n).
method_FROZEN.recovery_factor: recovery_factor(n) = eff_req(n) / |monthly LIQUID SUE effect|. The multiple by which a day-stamped announcement-window effect must exceed the attenuated monthly effect to clear |t|=2 at horizon H.
method_FROZEN.sign_hurdle: if the monthly LIQUID SUE effect is <= 0 (high-SUE underperforms at monthly resolution), the daily positive-PEAD thesis requires day-stamping to FLIP the liquid sign, not merely amplify it. Flag sign_contingent=true in that case. Magnitude (recovery_factor) is then NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT.

honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.summary: L3 already found the LIQUID monthly SUE effect is significantly NEGATIVE (high-SUE underperforms; -1.3%/mo, t=-3.2). Expect the event-level monthly SUE effect (positive-minus-negative SUE) to corroborate this: ~0 or NEGATIVE in the LIQUID universe, possibly mildly positive in ALL (microcap). So the daily-PEAD positive-edge thesis is SIGN-CONTINGENT: day-stamping must do more than amplify a weak positive monthly drift -- it must REVERSE the adverse monthly liquid sign. The required per-event effect for |t|=2 at the L9 rate (~95/yr) over 1-8 yr will be on the order of a few tens of bp to ~1% per event (sd_event ~ a few % monthly); the recovery factor will be finite but the SIGN hurdle dominates. HONEST caveat: the daily announcement-window effect is UNMEASURABLE offline -- L10 can only bound the requirement and flag the sign, it cannot confirm a positive daily drift exists. That confirmation needs the approved day-stamp fetch. Possible surprise: if event-level LIQUID monthly effect comes out positive (contradicting L3's tercile result), the sign hurdle vanishes and the thesis reverts to a pure magnitude/recovery question.
honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.most_likely_verdict: SIGN-CONTINGENT-FEASIBILITY-VIEW. A data-grounded statement that the decisive uncertainty for forward-data #1 is the daily-window SIGN (resolvable ONLY by the approved fetch), not volume (L9 settled) and not merely magnitude. NO edge claim; result recorded whatever it is.
